verses.text: Men ought 'always to pray, and not to faint'; the unjust judge yields to the widow's persistence. Persevering prayer. Keep asking, and do not lose heart.
Men ought 'always to pray, and not to faint'; the unjust judge yields to the widow's persistence. Persevering prayer. Keep asking, and do not lose heart.

Shall not God avenge His elect that cry to Him? 'when the Son of man cometh, shall he find faith?' A searching question. Will He find a praying, trusting people?
Shall not God avenge His elect that cry to Him? 'when the Son of man cometh, shall he find faith?' A searching question. Will He find a praying, trusting people?
Shall not God avenge His elect that cry to Him? 'when the Son of man cometh, shall he find faith?' A searching question. Will He find a praying, trusting people?
A Pharisee prays, thanking God he is not as others, boasting of his fasting and tithes. Proud religion. He trusts in himself and despises others.

The publican smites his breast, 'God be merciful to me a sinner'; this man went home justified. Humble plea. The contrite, not the proud, is accepted.
The publican smites his breast, 'God be merciful to me a sinner'; this man went home justified. Humble plea. The contrite, not the proud, is accepted.
Infants are brought; 'suffer little children to come unto me'; the kingdom is for such. Childlike faith. The lowly and trusting enter the kingdom.
Infants are brought; 'suffer little children to come unto me'; the kingdom is for such. Childlike faith. The lowly and trusting enter the kingdom.
Infants are brought; 'suffer little children to come unto me'; the kingdom is for such. Childlike faith. The lowly and trusting enter the kingdom.
A ruler asks the way to eternal life; Jesus names the commandments. The law's mirror. He shows the seeker God's holy standard.
A ruler asks the way to eternal life; Jesus names the commandments. The law's mirror. He shows the seeker God's holy standard.
A ruler asks the way to eternal life; Jesus names the commandments. The law's mirror. He shows the seeker God's holy standard.
Claiming to keep all, he is told, 'sell all... and follow me'; he is sorrowful, being rich. Riches refused. His wealth held him back from Christ.
Claiming to keep all, he is told, 'sell all... and follow me'; he is sorrowful, being rich. Riches refused. His wealth held him back from Christ.
Claiming to keep all, he is told, 'sell all... and follow me'; he is sorrowful, being rich. Riches refused. His wealth held him back from Christ.
How hardly the rich enter! 'the things which are impossible with men are possible with God.' Grace alone. Only God can save where riches bind the heart.
How hardly the rich enter! 'the things which are impossible with men are possible with God.' Grace alone. Only God can save where riches bind the heart.

We left all; none who forsakes for the kingdom but receives 'manifold more,' and life everlasting. Rich reward. No loss for Christ goes unrepaid.

He foretells the Son of man delivered, mocked, slain, and rising; they understand none of it. Veiled to them. They cannot yet grasp a suffering Saviour.

A blind man cries, 'Son of David, have mercy'; rebuked, he cries the more. Persistent faith. He will not be hushed from seeking Christ.
A blind man cries, 'Son of David, have mercy'; rebuked, he cries the more. Persistent faith. He will not be hushed from seeking Christ.

A blind man cries, 'Son of David, have mercy'; rebuked, he cries the more. Persistent faith. He will not be hushed from seeking Christ.
'What wilt thou?' 'that I may receive my sight'; 'thy faith hath saved thee'; he sees and follows, glorifying God. Sight and praise. The healed becomes a worshipping follower.
